description_long: This object is a detailed model of a slipper lobster, characterized by its broad, flattened cephalothorax and segmented abdomen. The carapace is rough and spiny, with a reddish-brown coloration and lighter patches, giving it a rugged, armored appearance. The front of the body is wide and shield-like, tapering towards the rear, with several short, robust walking legs extending from the underside. The tail is fan-shaped and fringed, typical of many lobsters, and the antennae are short and thick, projecting forward from the head. No large claws are present, distinguishing it from true lobsters. The surface texture appears hard and rough, with a matte finish that mimics the exoskeleton of a real crustacean.
description: A reddish-brown slipper lobster with a broad, spiny carapace, segmented body, and short legs, lacking large claws. Its surface is rough and armored, with a fan-shaped tail and short antennae.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the body, segmented abdomen, and short legs, with the broad carapace and fan-shaped tail visible.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the wide, shield-like cephalothorax, short antennae, and the rough, spiny texture of the carapace.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the segmented abdomen, short walking legs, and the overall flattened profile of the lobster.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the fan-shaped tail, the arrangement of the legs, and the tapering body structure.
